channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Prayers and tears mark 20 years since the Indian Ocean tsunami that killed some 230,000 people
item.description: BANDA ACEH, Indonesia (AP) — People gathered in prayer and visited mass graves in Indonesia’s Aceh province on Thursday to mark 20 years since the massive Indian Ocean tsunami hit the region in one of modern history’s worst natural disasters.
item.dc:creator: REZA SAIFULLAH and EDNA TARIGAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: JonBenet Ramsey’s father plans 'important meeting' with Boulder police chief, DNA lab rep
item.description: JonBenet Ramsey's father, John Ramsey, says he plans to meet with the Boulder, Colorado, police chief next month to discuss the possibility of testing crime-scene evidence for DNA.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Most disastrous freak accidents of 2024: 7 times everyday fun turned fatal
item.description: From a deadly skydiving accident to a terrifying pool electrocution, here are several freak accidents that astonished audiences in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Defiant victims who turned the tables on attackers in 2024
item.description: Crime victims across the country this year took it upon themselves to fight back against their attackers, with video capturing how the incidents went down.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese automaker BYD slams reports of poor conditions at a factory site in Brazil
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A spokesperson for Chinese automaker BYD has objected to reports about poor conditions at a construction site in Brazil where it is building a factory, saying the allegations were aimed at “smearing” China and Chinese brands.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Former Presidential candidate in Taiwan, Ko Wen-je, indicted for corruption
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Prosecutors in Taiwan indicted former presidential candidate and Taiwan People's Party founder Ko Wen-je on corruption charges Thursday, accusing him of taking bribes during his time as mayor of the island's capital.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU and JOHNSON LAI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Japan Airlines was hit by a cyberattack, delaying flights during the year-end holiday season
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan Airlines said it was hit by a cyberattack Thursday, causing delays to more than 20 domestic flights but the carrier said it was able to stop the onslaught and restore its systems hours later. There was no impact on flight safety, it said.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump announces ambassador to Panama amid fight over canal
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced Miami-Dade County Commissioner Kevin Marino Cabrera as his pick for U.S. ambassador to Panama. The announcement comes as Trump is sparking a feud with the U.S. ally over control of the Panama Canal.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tells 37 people with sentences commuted by Biden to 'go to hell' in lengthy Christmas post
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday used a lengthy Christmas Day social media post to tell the 37 people who had their death sentences commuted by President Biden to "go to hell.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill Bergey, a Pro Bowl linebacker for the Eagles, dies at 79
item.description: Bill Bergey, a five-time Pro Bowl linebacker who played in a Super Bowl for the Philadelphia Eagles, died Wednesday, the team announced. He was 79.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trial of man accused in Trump assassination attempt in Florida pushed back to September
item.description: A man accused of attempting to assassinate President-elect Donald Trump in South Florida won’t be tried until September 2025.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Azerbaijani airliner with 67 people onboard crashes in Kazakhstan leaving 32 survivors
item.description: An Azerbaijani airliner with 67 people onboard crashed Wednesday near the Kazakhstani city of Aktau, leaving at least 32 survivors, according to officials. More than 30 people may be dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Mega Millions: Jackpot now over $1 billion
item.description: The Mega Millions jackpot has only been won three times in 2024, the fewest wins in a single year since the game began.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KTLA 5

item.title: Stunning photos show lava erupting from Hawaii's Kilauea volcano
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — One of the world's most active volcanoes spewed lava into the air for a second straight day on Tuesday. The eruption of Kilauea volcano on Hawaii's Big Island has stayed within the mountain's summit caldera inside Hawaii Volcanoes National Park. No homes were threatened.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The end of the 'Rust' criminal case against Alec Baldwin may unlock a civil lawsuit
item.description: The conclusion of a criminal case against Alec Baldwin in the fatal shooting of a cinematographer clears the way for a related civil lawsuit by relatives of the deceased woman and efforts to depose the actor under oath, attorneys for plaintiffs in the civil suit said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: This holiday tradition is hoping for a Christmas miracle. Here’s why
item.description: It’s become synonymous with the other sounds of the season: Mariah Carey’s Christmas music or the doorbell dings of the Amazon delivery drivers—yes, the ringing of those Salvation Army Kettle Bells. But if you’ve noticed fewer bell ringers this year, you’re not wrong.
item.dc:creator: Peter Wilgoren, KTLA 5

item.title: Biden signs a bill officially making the bald eagle the national bird of the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The bald eagle, a symbol of the power and strength of the United States for more than 240 years, earned an overdue honor on Tuesday: It officially became the country's national bird.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden takes heat for vetoing bill that would add new judgeships
item.description: Federal judiciary leadership and some lawmakers are criticizing President Biden for vetoing a bill that would’ve added 66 new federal judgeships amid growing caseloads.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Eggs added to FDA's updated healthy claim food list
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration has added eggs to its updated list of healthy foods along with fish and vegetables
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mega Millions: Jackpot now over $1 billion
item.description: The Mega Millions jackpot has only been won three times in 2024, the fewest wins in a single year since the game began.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Opinion: Elon Musk wants to ‘delete’ many Americans' financial lifeline
item.description: It is especially surprising that one of the first federal agencies to come under scrutiny from the incoming administration is one that has returned billions of dollars to many of the same consumers who were counting on leaders in Washington to look out for their wallets.
item.dc:creator: Sharon McGowan, opinion contributor, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Why Netflix is streaming NFL games on Christmas Day
item.description: To make the events even more enthralling, Mariah Carey is set to perform ahead of kick-off while Beyoncé is taking over halftime of the Ravens-Texans game.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Eye drops recalled nationwide due to fungal contamination
item.description: The recall was issued after a consumer complained of a "foreign material" they found in a sealed vial of the eye drops.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, NewsNation

item.title: Major storm pounds California's central coast, blamed for man's death and partially collapsing pier
item.description: SANTA CRUZ, Calif. (AP) — A major storm pounded California’s central coast on Monday, bringing flooding and high surf that was blamed for fatally trapping a man beneath debris on a beach and later partially collapsing a pier, tossing three people into the Pacific Ocean.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA MENDOZA and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Officer fatally shot in a North Carolina supermarket, suspect in custody, police say
item.description: GREENSBORO, N.C. (AP) — A police officer responding to a report of a man with a gun inside a North Carolina supermarket was fatally shot Monday and a suspect was later taken into custody, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sean Manaea to re-sign with Mets for $75 million over 3 years, AP source says
item.description: Sean Manaea is set to return to the New York Mets on a $75 million, three-year contract, a person familiar with the agreement told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pennsylvania police announce arrest of The Grinch in holiday video
item.description: Dressed in his Santa suit and ready to cause some mischief, The Grinch managed to make off with someone's gifts.
item.dc:creator: Drew Miller, NewsNation

item.title: Bill Clinton is hospitalized with a fever but in good spirits, spokesperson says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former President Bill Clinton was admitted Monday to MedStar Georgetown University Hospital in Washington after developing a fever. The 78-year-old was hospitalized in the “afternoon for testing and observation," Angel Urena, Clinton's deputy chief of staff, said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trial for suspect in second Trump assassination attempt delayed
item.description: Lawyers for the suspect, Ryan Routh, sought a delay of the scheduled Feb. 10 trial date, citing the large volume of evidence in the case.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Bill Clinton admitted to hospital with fever
item.description: Former President Bill Clinton was admitted to a Washington hospital on Monday with a fever, his spokesperson said on social media.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Biden gives life in prison to 37 of 40 federal death row inmates before Trump can resume executions
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday announced that he is commuting the sentences of 37 of the 40 people on federal death row, converting their punishments to life imprisonment just weeks before President-elect Donald Trump, an outspoken proponent of expanding capital punishment, takes of
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Luigi Mangione's lawyer blasts New York officials for 'staged' perp walk
item.description: The attorney for Luigi Mangione, who has been accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, slammed New York City officials for what she referred to as a “staged” perp walk for her client last week.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, ABC 4

item.title: Adnan Syed's lawyers seek to reduce his prison sentence
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — Attorneys for Adnan Syed, whose complex legal case was chronicled in the hit podcast “Serial," are seeking to have his prison sentence reduced as he awaits further court decisions.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
